content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
July 19, 1995

Michael B. Mount, Esq.
Chief Counsel
Department of Developmental Services
1600 9th Street
Post Office Box 94244-2020
Sacramento, California 94402-2020

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-95-225

Dear Mr. Mount:

This letter responds to your request for formal written
advice regarding the mass mailing prohibitions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

May the name and seal of both Governor Wilson and
Superintendent of Public Instruction Delaine Eastin appear on
brochures regarding California's Early Start Program which will be
distributed to various local agencies, doctors' offices and
nonprofit groups for further distribution to individuals?

CONCLUSION

Yes. The names and seals of both public officials may be
placed on the brochures in question, because the brochures do not
qualify as prohibited mass mailings under the Act.

FACTS

The Department of Developmental Services (the "Department")
is preparing brochures for the Early Start Program, a program of
early intervention services for infants and toddlers with
disabilities. As part of the education drive associated with this
program, the Department will provide these brochures to 21
regional centers, 116 special education local plan areas and 32
family resource centers. These agencies will disseminate the
brochures to families who request services from the program and to
health care providers who make referrals to the program. It is
anticipated that these agencies will disseminate the brochures to
other providers and organizations as part of their outreach and

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18995.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-225
Page 2

child find efforts.  Additionally, the Department will provide
copies of the brochures to anyone who specifically requests them.

ANALYSIS

Mass mailings sent at government expense are prohibited under
the Act. (Section 8900.)  Under Commission Regulation 18901, a
mass mailing is defined as more than 200 substantially similar
tangible items delivered in a calendar month, by any means, to
recipients at their residence, place of employment, business, or
post office box.  A mass mailing does not include any item sent in
response to an unsolicited request.  If the distribution of any
item does not meet this basic definition, it is not a prohibited
mass mailing and further analysis of the possible exceptions
provided under the law is not warranted.

In the Anaya Advice Letter, No. A-91-215, the Commission
stated that brochures sent out to public locations for recipients
to pick up on their own, or handed out in public areas, are not
subject to the mass mailing provisions of the Act.  However, the
letter went on to state that the public agency should take some
precautions to ensure that the mass mailing prohibition is not
violated by the redistribution of the item to over 200 recipients
at their homes, places of employment or post office boxes.
(Citing the Lavagetto Advice Letter, No. A-90-199.)  This advice
was recently restated in the Gladwell Advice Letter, No. A-95-162.
Accordingly, the brochures for the Early Start Program would not
be subject to the mass mailing prohibition of the Act if
distribution by the Department was limited to the 21 regional
centers, 116 special education local plan areas, 32 family
resource centers, and to individuals pursuant to an unsolicited
request.  It may be appropriate for the Department to communicate
to the organizations and agencies that receive bulk quantities of
these brochures, that the unsolicited redistribution of over 200
brochures per month directly to a recipient's home, business,
place of employment or post office box, would violate the mass
mailing prohibition of the Act.

We trust that this advice fully responds to your request.  If
you should have any additional questions, please do not hesitate
to contact the undersigned at (916) 322-5785.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By:  Daniel E. Muallem
     Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:DEM:ak

Superintendent of Public Instruction Delaine Eastin appear on
brochures regarding California’s Early Start Program which will be
distributed to various local agencies, doctors’ offices and
nonprofit groups for further distribution to individuals?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
The names and seals of both public officials may be
placed on the brochures in question,
